items.front: 6-1-1: Prerequisite for mileage-based (DME and/or ATD) procedures
items.back: Direct pilot/controller VHF or UHF voice communications must be maintained.

items.front: Why can't mileage-based separation be used when voice communications are relayed?
items.back: 6-1-1 requires DIRECT pilot/controller VHF or UHF voice communications. Relayed communications do not meet the requirement.

items.front: 6-1-2: Deadline for obtaining a missing position report affecting separation
items.back: No later than 5 minutes after the aircraft was estimated over the fix.

items.front: Which missed position reports trigger the 6-1-2 action requirement?
items.back: Only position reports that affect separation. Reports not affecting separation do not trigger the requirement.

items.front: 6-1-3: Rule on duplicate position reports
items.back: Do not require an aircraft to make the same position report to more than one facility. Applies regardless of facility type (tower, center, approach control).

items.front: 6-1-4a: Terminal adjacent-airport wake minima — behind Category A
items.back: Category B, C, D, E, F, G, H, or I behind A = 3 minutes.

items.front: 6-1-4a: Terminal adjacent-airport wake minima — behind Category B or D
items.back: Category B, C, D, E, F, G, H, or I behind B or D = 2 minutes.

items.front: 6-1-4a: Terminal adjacent-airport wake minima — behind Category C
items.back: Category E, F, G, H, or I behind C = 2 minutes.

items.front: 6-1-4a: Terminal adjacent-airport wake minima — behind Category E
items.back: Category I behind E = 2 minutes.

items.front: 6-1-4b: En route adjacent-airport wake minima — behind super
items.back: Heavy, large, or small behind super = 3 minutes.

items.front: 6-1-4b: En route adjacent-airport wake minima — behind heavy
items.back: Heavy, large, or small behind heavy = 2 minutes.

items.front: 6-1-4b: En route adjacent-airport wake minima — behind B757
items.back: Small behind B757 = 2 minutes.

items.front: 6-1-5a: Terminal arrivals — which runway configurations do the minima apply to?
items.back: Same runway; parallel runway separated by less than 2,500 feet; or crossing runway if projected flight paths will cross.

items.front: 6-1-5a1: Terminal arrivals, behind Category A
items.back: Category B, C, D, E, F, or G behind A = 3 minutes.

items.front: 6-1-5a2: Terminal arrivals, H or I behind Category A
items.back: Category H or I behind A = 4 minutes (longest terminal minimum).

items.front: 6-1-5a3: Terminal arrivals, behind Category B or D
items.back: Category B, C, D, E, F, or G behind B or D = 2 minutes.

items.front: 6-1-5a4: Terminal arrivals, behind Category C
items.back: Category E, F, or G behind C = 2 minutes.

items.front: 6-1-5a5: Terminal arrivals, H or I behind Category B, C, or D
items.back: Category H or I behind B, C, or D = 3 minutes.

items.front: 6-1-5a6: Terminal arrivals, I behind Category E
items.back: Category I behind E = 3 minutes.

items.front: 6-1-5b: En route arrivals to same runway — behind super
items.back: Heavy or large = 3 min; Small = 4 min.

items.front: 6-1-5b: En route arrivals to same runway — behind heavy
items.back: Heavy or large = 2 min; Small = 3 min.

items.front: 6-1-5b: En route arrivals to same runway — behind B757
items.back: Small behind B757 = 3 minutes.

items.front: 6-1-5c: En route arrivals parallel (<2500 ft) or crossing runway — behind super
items.back: Heavy, large, or small behind super = 3 minutes.

items.front: 6-1-5c: En route arrivals parallel (<2500 ft) or crossing runway — behind heavy
items.back: Heavy, large, or small behind heavy = 2 minutes.

items.front: 6-1-5c: En route arrivals parallel (<2500 ft) or crossing runway — behind B757
items.back: Small behind B757 = 2 minutes (different from same-runway 3 min in 6-1-5b3).

items.front: 6-1-5b vs 6-1-5c: What is the key operational distinction?
items.back: 6-1-5b = same runway arrivals. 6-1-5c = parallel runways separated by less than 2,500 ft, or crossing runways where projected flight paths cross.
